title: Iran Fires Back at Trump: 'We Are One Soul, Not Divided!'
summary: President Trump has been saying that Iran's leaders are fighting with each other — that there are "moderates" (people who want peace) and "hardliners" (people who want to fight). But Iran's top leaders just gave a big, united answer: "We are all one soul!" Iran's president, foreign minister, and parliament speaker all posted the same message on social media. It said there are no radicals or moderates in Iran — just Iranians who are united behind their Supreme Leader. This comes after a tough time for Iran. In February, the U.S. and Israel killed Iran's former Supreme Leader, Ali Khamenei. His son, Mojtaba Khamenei, is now the leader, but he hasn't been seen in public since. Meanwhile, the U.S. has a blockade (a military barrier) around Iran's ports, stopping ships from coming and going. Iran has also closed the Strait of Hormuz, a narrow waterway where a lot of the world's oil travels. This has made oil prices go up. Trump says the blockade is "airtight" (completely sealed) and that Iran's military is destroyed. But Iran says it is united and ready to defend itself. Israel says it's waiting for Trump's permission to attack Iran again. So, is Iran really divided? Or is it standing strong together?
why_it_matters: This story shows how countries can have very different stories about what's happening, and it reminds you that war and blockades affect things like the price of gas and the safety of people far away.

keywords.explanation: people who want to fight rather than make peace
people who want peace and compromise
the highest religious and political leader of Iran
a military barrier that stops ships from going to or from a country
a narrow waterway where a lot of the world's oil travels

options: A united message saying 'We are all one soul'
questions.correct_answer: A united message saying 'We are all one soul'

options: He was killed by U.S. and Israel
questions.correct_answer: He was killed by U.S. and Israel

questions.question: Who is the new leader of Iran?
Why are oil prices going up?
options: Because Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz
Because the U.S. stopped buying oil

questions.question: What did Trump say about the blockade?
Who said Israel is waiting for Trump's permission to attack Iran?

background_read: Iran and the United States have been enemies for many decades, and they often disagree about politics and power in the Middle East.
The Strait of Hormuz is a very important waterway because a lot of oil from Middle Eastern countries passes through it.
When one country attacks another's leader, it often creates big changes and tensions.
Article_Structure: WHO: Iran's top leaders (president, foreign minister, parliament speaker) and Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei
WHAT: They posted a unified message rejecting Trump's claims of division
WHERE: On social media (X); the conflict involves the Strait of Hormuz and Iran's ports
WHY: To show they are united after a U.S.-Israeli strike killed the former Supreme Leader

perspectives.description: They want to show that despite the attacks, the country is not divided and stands together.
They say Iran is weak and divided, and they threaten more military action.
Countries that rely on oil from the Middle East are worried because closing the Strait of Hormuz raises prices.
